title: Mass Timber Federal Buildings Act of 2025
short_title: Mass Timber Federal Buildings Act of 2025
plain_summary: This new law says the US government must choose buildings made from special American wood products when they build or rent federal buildings. The government also has to study how these wood buildings help fight climate change. When picking wood, they should prefer wood that comes from forest restoration projects and from forest owners who need more help. This law is meant to help American wood companies and protect the environment at the same time.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Mass Timber Federal Buildings Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2. Made in America Mass Timber
sections.text:
This section explains what certain words mean and sets up new rules for government buildings. The Administrator means the person who runs General Services. An innovative wood product is a special type of wood product as defined in a twenty eighteen farm law. An innovative wood product facility is a place that makes these special wood products as defined in a two thousand two farm law. Mass timber is a type of building material made from wood as defined in the twenty eighteen farm law. A public building is any building owned by the government, including military bases. Restoration practices are ways of managing forests that help fix or improve how forests work by changing the types and arrangement of trees and making forests healthier and stronger.

When the government wants to build, change, buy, or rent a public building, they must give priority to buildings that use innovative wood products as much as possible. These wood products must come from facilities located in the United States and be harvested from forests in the United States from responsible sources. Responsible sources means the wood was produced following an independent certification standard or comes from places with good forest management programs, including federal, state, and tribal lands.

When buying these innovative wood products, the government must consider different contract options and give preference to wood products that come from forest restoration work, forest management done to protect communities from wildfires, or from underserved forest owners like tribes and small family forests. The government must ask for proper documentation to prove the wood comes from these preferred sources.

Within one hundred eighty days after this law is passed, the Administrator must work with the Secretary of Agriculture to study the environmental impact of new public buildings that use innovative wood products. This study must look at the global warming effects of the entire building process and follow international standards. Within one hundred eighty days after finishing this study, the Administrator must send a report to Congress and make it available to the public explaining what they found.
